checks.name: lint
checks.cmd: python3 -m py_compile scripts/workflow_validate.py scripts/workflow_validate_core.py scripts/workflow_checks.py scripts/workflow_checks_core.py scripts/workflow_detect.py scripts/workflow_utils.py scripts/workflow_render.py scripts/workflow_hooks.py scripts/workflow_memory.py

principles.name: Think Before Coding
principles.notes: Full discuss->plan->implement cycle with CONTEXT.json, 3 plans with explicit goals and verify steps

principles.name: Simplicity First
principles.notes: Hooks are simple single-purpose scripts; reconcile is straightforward close-if-done logic; no speculative abstractions

principles.name: Surgical Changes
principles.notes: 22 files changed, all directly related to compaction-resilience; no drive-by refactors; each plan touched exactly its declared files

principles.name: Goal-Driven Execution
principles.notes: Each plan had explicit verify commands (py_compile, CLI invocation, assertion checks); all passed; feature self-validated during Plan 03 execution

principles.name: Prefer shared utility packages over hand-rolled helpers
principles.notes: Reconcile uses existing memory engine APIs (close, show, is_initialized); hooks use existing workflow_memory.py CLI

principles.name: Don't probe data YOLO-style
principles.notes: Uses explicit schemas: WorktreeSession dataclass, checkpoint schemaVersion, structured JSON contracts

principles.name: Validate boundaries
principles.notes: Memory ID regex validation in bridge.py; JSON parse error handling in all hooks; graceful exception handling throughout

principles.name: Typed SDKs
principles.notes: No external APIs involved; uses internal Python APIs with dataclass typing

manualFindings.detail: No injection risks: all subprocess.run calls use explicit arg lists (no shell=True); memory ID validated via regex before use; no secrets exposed in checkpoint; hooks always exit 0
Additive-only changes to bridge.py, __init__.py, workflow_memory.py; new modules (reconcile.py, hooks) follow existing patterns; checkpoint has schemaVersion for future compat
All hooks always exit 0 (non-blocking); subprocess timeouts (3s) prevent hangs; reconcile returns structured error summary; atomic checkpoint writes via tempfile+os.replace
All 22 changed files are directly related to compaction-resilience; no unrelated refactors
No unit tests for hooks or reconcile module; validated via py_compile and real-world execution during Plan 03 (reconciled 2 orphaned issues successfully)
.cnogo/compaction-checkpoint.json is runtime state but not gitignored; similar files (worktree-session.json, command-usage.jsonl) are already gitignored

warnings: No unit tests for hooks/reconcile — acceptable given real-world validation during Plan 03 and stdlib-only Python
.cnogo/compaction-checkpoint.json should be added to .gitignore (runtime state, not source)

evidence: Operating principles checked: Think Before Coding, Simplicity First, Surgical Changes, Goal-Driven Execution, Prefer shared utility packages over hand-rolled helpers, Don't probe data YOLO-style, Validate boundaries
Automated checks: lint: skipped, types: skipped, tests: skipped
Manual review areas: security, contracts, failure-behavior, scope-hygiene, test-quality, gitignore
